The classification of this plant includes its kingdom as Plantae, and its phylum as Angiospermophyta. Further classification places it in the class Magnoliopsida and the order Santalales. There is no information available on the plant "Eyrythalia dichotoma". However, I can provide a paragraph on a different plant, "Eyebright" (Erythraea centaurium or Euphrasia officinalis or Euphrasia hirtella). 

Eyebright is a flowering plant, also known as Erythraea centaurium, that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat eye and respiratory infections, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Eyebright include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Eyebright include teas, tinctures, and eye washes, which are often used to treat conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and other eye conditions.
